EC missed out the Lost Deposit Association for the PAS Candidate for DUN Sungai Nibong. Constituency Spelling follows the 1974 EC Maps of Peninsular Malaysia. Total electorates from Tindak Malaysia's computation is 848,885, but in the EC appendix is 848,855. Tindak compilation may be correct, and the EC appendix may be due to misprinting/typo. There are two sets of interpretations of the seats allocated to UMNO and Berjasa if one compares Harold Crouch's publication with the New Straits Times, 7th April 1982. Refer to the notes below for the discrepancy. According to New Straits Times 19/4/1982, Independent Candidate (Tahar) of DUN Salor joined BN-UMNO after nominations. There are a number of candidates were sex category is not ascertained and significant gaps for the candidate age. Created on: According to Harold Crouch, after nomination, UMNO contested 205 seats and 11 Berjasa seats because of rejected nominations. Tindak Malaysia has established that UMNO candidates were disqualified for DUN Bintong (Perak) and DUN Selising (Kelantan). Tindak Malaysia has established two Berjasa Candidates were disqualified for DUN Kemumin and DUN Salor

According to Harold Crouch, Berjasa was given 12 seats in Kelantan (contested) and one state seat in Perak. Zakry Abadi's 'Mahathir' Machiavelli (1990) indicated the contestant fof BN in DUN Kuala Kurau (Perak) is from Berjasa
